records.ety: [OE. botum, botme, AS. botm; akin to OS. bodom, D. bodem, OHG. podam, G. boden, Icel. botn, Sw. botten, Dan. bund (for budn), L. fundus (for fudnus), Gr. pyqmh`n (for fyqmh`n), Skr. budhna (for bhudhna), and Ir. bonn sole of the foot, W. bon stem, base. √257. Cf. 4th Found, Fund, n.]

defs: The lowest part of anything; the foot; as, the bottom of a tree or well; the bottom of a hill, a lane, or a page.
quotes: Or dive into the bottom of the deep.

defs: The part of anything which is beneath the contents and supports them, as the part of a chair on which a person sits, the circular base or lower head of a cask or tub, or the plank floor of a ship's hold; the under surface.
quotes: Barrels with the bottom knocked out.
No two chairs were alike; such high backs and low backs and leather bottoms and worsted bottoms.

defs: That upon which anything rests or is founded, in a literal or a figurative sense; foundation; groundwork.
The bed of a body of water, as of a river, lake, sea.
Low land formed by alluvial deposits along a river; low-lying ground; a dale; a valley.
The part of a ship which is ordinarily under water; hence, the vessel itself; a ship.
quotes: My ventures are not in one bottom trusted.
Not to sell the teas, but to return them to London in the same bottoms in which they were shipped.

defs: Power of endurance; as, a horse of a good bottom.

quotes: He was at the bottom of many excellent counsels.

defs: Of or pertaining to the bottom; fundamental; lowest; under; as, bottom rock; the bottom board of a wagon box; bottom prices.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bottomed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bottoming.]

defs: To found or build upon; to fix upon as a support; -- followed by on or upon.
quotes: Action is supposed to be bottomed upon principle.
Those false and deceiving grounds upon which many bottom their eternal state].

defs: To furnish with a bottom; as, to bottom a chair.
To reach or get to the bottom of.
To rest, as upon an ultimate support; to be based or grounded; -- usually with on or upon.
quotes: Find on what foundation any proposition bottoms.

defs: To reach or impinge against the bottom, so as to impede free action, as when the point of a cog strikes the bottom of a space between two other cogs, or a piston the end of a cylinder.

records.ety: [OE. botme, perh. corrupt. for button. See Button.]

defs: A ball or skein of thread; a cocoon.
quotes: Silkworms finish their bottoms in . . . fifteen days.

defs: To wind round something, as in making a ball of thread.
quotes: As you unwind her love from him, Lest it should ravel and be good to none, You must provide to bottom it on me.
